 {"id":"phage_explorer-10p","title":"[WEB-P2] Core Visualization: Canvas Rendering Engine","description":"# Phase 2: Core Visualization (Target: Weeks 3-5)

## Purpose
Build the high-performance rendering engine that displays sequence data at 60fps. This is the visual heart of the application.

## Why This Matters
The sequence grid is the most frequently viewed component. Users scroll through thousands of bases constantly. Any frame drops or latency destroys the "instant feedback" experience that defines Phage Explorer.

## Design Philosophy
We render to Canvas 2D (not DOM) because:
1. **Performance**: DOM cannot achieve 60fps with 5000+ colored elements
2. **Control**: Pixel-perfect cyberpunk aesthetics
3. **Consistency**: Same visual output across browsers

WebGL is used for post-processing effects (glow, scanlines) that would be expensive in 2D.

## Deliverables
1. **Canvas Sequence Grid Renderer** - Main rendering engine
2. **Glyph Atlas** - Pre-rendered character sprites
3. **Virtual Scrolling** - Efficient rendering of 100k+ sequences
4. **Gene Map Renderer** - Canvas/SVG hybrid
5. **Sparkline Components** - Ported from TUI
6. **WebGL Post-Processing** - Shaders for effects

## Technical Architecture

### SequenceGridRenderer
```typescript
- OffscreenCanvas double-buffering
- High-DPI support (devicePixelRatio)
- Dirty region tracking for partial updates
- Color batching (group characters by color)
- requestAnimationFrame scheduling
```

### GlyphAtlas
```typescript
- Pre-render all nucleotides (ACGTN) + amino acids
- Single canvas texture for all characters
- drawGlyph() copies from atlas to destination
- ~10x faster than fillText() per character
```

### Virtual Scrolling
```typescript
- Only render visible rows + overscan
- Smooth scroll with momentum physics
- Prefetch ahead of scroll direction
- 60fps target with 100k+ bases
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 60fps scrolling on 100k+ base sequences
- [ ] All nucleotide/amino acid colors match TUI
- [ ] Gene map shows correct positions and strands
- [ ] Scanlines and glow effects render correctly
- [ ] High-DPI displays render crisply
- [ ] Memory usage stable during extended scrolling

## Performance Targets
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Frame time | <16.67ms |
| First paint | <100ms |
| Scroll latency | <8ms |
| Memory (100k seq) | <50MB |

## Risks & Mitigations
- **Risk**: Canvas performance varies across browsers
  - **Mitigation**: Test Safari, use conservative techniques
- **Risk**: Glyph atlas memory
  - **Mitigation**: Generate at runtime, don't ship as image
- **Risk**: WebGL shader complexity
  - **Mitigation**: Simple shaders first, optimize later

## Dependencies
- Phase 0 (app scaffold with theme system)
- Phase 1 (database for sequence data)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-07T21:56:14.282689Z","updated_at":"2025-12-07T23:04:37.240754Z","closed_at":"2025-12-07T23:04:37.240754Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-10p","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-87z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T22:04:30.294827Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-10p","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-pml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T22:04:31.525345Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-1cwn","title":"Task: Audit and fix all touch targets to 44px minimum","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:32:37.488564187Z","updated_at":"2025-12-12T22:52:22.322091771Z","closed_at":"2025-12-12T22:52:22.322091771Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-1cwn","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-y5wb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:33:16.512172478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-1cwn","author":"ubuntu","text":"# Audit and Fix Touch Targets to 44px Minimum

## WCAG 2.5.5 Requirement
Target Size (Level AAA): Touch targets must be at least 44x44 CSS pixels.

## Audit Methodology

### Step 1: Identify All Interactive Elements
```bash
# Find all clickable elements in web package
grep -r "onClick\|onPress\|role=\"button\"\|<button" packages/web/src --include="*.tsx"
```

### Step 2: Measure Current Sizes
Use browser DevTools to measure:
- Visual size (what user sees)
- Hit area (padding + element size)
- Spacing between targets

### Step 3: Document Violations
Create spreadsheet with:
| Component | Element | Current Size | Required | Gap |
|-----------|---------|--------------|----------|-----|
| Header | Menu btn | 32x32 | 44x44 | 12px |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

## Known Violations

### From Audit
1. **Badge buttons** - Often 24-32px
2. **Compact mode buttons** - Reduced for density
3. **Gene map segments** - Variable, often < 44px wide
4. **Overlay close buttons** - Sometimes 32px
5. **List item actions** - Small icons without padding

## Fix Strategy

### Option 1: Increase Visual Size
Make button visually larger.
```css
.btn-sm {
  min-height: 44px;
  min-width: 44px;
}
```

### Option 2: Expand Hit Area (Preferred)
Keep visual size, expand padding:
```css
.btn-sm {
  min-height: 32px; /* Visual */
  padding: 6px;     /* Hit area expands by 12px */
  /* Total hit area: 44px */
}
```

### Option 3: Invisible Hit Expander
For icons that must stay small:
```tsx
<button className="icon-btn">
  <span className="hit-area-expander" /> {/* 44x44 invisible */}
  <Icon size={20} />
</button>
```

## Files to Modify
- `packages/web/src/styles/mobile-overrides.css`
- `packages/web/src/styles/components.css`
- Individual components as needed

## Testing
- [ ] iPhone SE (smallest common screen)
- [ ] Test with fat-finger simulation
- [ ] Verify spacing between adjacent targets
- [ ] Screen reader reports correct bounds","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:34:20Z"},{"id":35,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-1cwn","author":"ubuntu","text":"IN PROGRESS (WhiteCreek): Taking this task. Will audit interactive elements and fix touch target sizes to meet 44px minimum.","created_at":"2025-12-12T22:50:35Z"}]}

Builds/tests: bun run --cwd packages/web build + bun run test = PASS.","created_at":"2025-12-12T17:24:25Z"},{"id":4,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-wzjl.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"More mobile/perf fixes: (1) Model3DView on-demand RAF now correctly keeps animating during OrbitControls damping by detecting camera pose deltas (OrbitControls.update() returns void), plus renderer opts alpha:false + powerPreference:high-performance; (2) structure-load effect no longer re-runs on renderMode changes (avoids double rebuild + unwanted camera refits); (3) swapped key heights to dvh units (SequenceView + 3D fullscreen) to reduce iOS Safari vh glitches; (4) added mobile background scroll-lock when a blocking overlay or phone list drawer is open. Builds/tests: bun run --cwd packages/web build + bun run test = PASS.","created_at":"2025-12-12T18:18:26Z"},{"id":5,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-wzjl.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Code-splitting/perf: App now lazy-loads Model3DView (Three.js) only when 3D is enabled, and shows a lightweight placeholder panel otherwise. - aria-live regions for dynamic content
- Focus indicators (focus-visible styling)
- High contrast mode support
- Reduced motion (prefers-reduced-motion)
- Forced colors mode support
- Screen reader utilities (.sr-only)
- ARIA labels on interactive elements

Remaining: Manual testing with screen readers (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver) and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance audit.","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:38:54Z"},{"id":105,"issue_id":"phage_explorer-wzjl.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed by BlackSnow.  {"id":"phage_explorer-wzjl.2.1","title":"A11y: LearnMenu keyboard + focus behavior","description":"### Background
`packages/web/src/components/LearnMenu.tsx` renders a popover menu but currently has limited keyboard behavior:
- no Esc-to-close
- no arrow-key navigation
- focus is not moved into the menu on open
- focus is not restored to the trigger on close

### Goal
Make LearnMenu behave like an accessible menu/popover:
- Enter/Space opens
- focus moves to the first item
- ArrowUp/ArrowDown roves through items
- Esc closes and returns focus to trigger
- Click-outside still closes

### Touchpoints
- `packages/web/src/components/LearnMenu.tsx`","design":"Implementation notes:
- Use a roving tabindex (only one menu item tabbable at a time) or a listbox pattern.
- Avoid focus traps: this is a popover, not a modal.","acceptance_criteria":"- LearnMenu is fully usable without a mouse.
- Focus behavior is predictable (open → focus first item; close → return focus to trigger).
- ARIA roles/attributes are correct (menu + menuitem or popover/listbox pattern).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-13T10:18:13.896392912Z","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:34:32.893515255Z","closed_at":"2025-12-13T10:34:32.893515255Z","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-wzjl.2.2","title":"A11y: Overlays return focus on close + consistent Esc behavior","description":"### Background
The app uses an overlay system (`packages/web/src/components/overlays/*`). For accessibility and premium UX, overlays should:
- close on Escape (where appropriate)
- return focus to the element that opened them
- avoid leaving focus on a removed node

Some modal focus work exists historically, but the current overlay set should be audited end-to-end.

### Goal
Add consistent focus/keyboard behavior across overlays.

### Touchpoints
- `packages/web/src/components/overlays/Overlay.tsx`
- `packages/web/src/components/overlays/OverlayProvider.tsx`
- `packages/web/src/components/overlays/OverlayManager.tsx`","design":"Implementation notes:
- Store a reference to `document.activeElement` when opening an overlay and restore on close.
- Consider a small helper to avoid duplicating logic across overlays.","acceptance_criteria":"- Closing an overlay returns focus to the triggering control.
- Escape closes overlays that are dismissible.
- Focus is not trapped unless the overlay is modal by design.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-13T10:18:31.083967850Z","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:58:12.747529292Z","closed_at":"2025-12-13T10:58:12.747529292Z","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-wzjl.2.3","title":"A11y: Glossary drawer/panel focus management","description":"### Background
Glossary is a core Beginner Mode surface. For accessibility and premium polish:
- Opening the glossary should land focus in a predictable place.
- Escape should close the glossary drawer/panel.
- Closing should return focus to the opener.

We already improved context-jump behavior inside `GlossaryPanel.tsx`, but container-level focus management still needs to be verified.

### Touchpoints
- `packages/web/src/education/glossary/GlossaryPanel.tsx`
- the glossary container/drawer component (where `openGlossary()` is implemented)","acceptance_criteria":"- Open glossary → focus moves to the search input or term list.
- Esc closes glossary.
- Close → focus returns to the element that opened the glossary.
- Screen readers announce the glossary region appropriately.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-13T10:18:47.335292515Z","updated_at":"2025-12-13T11:21:15.714672548Z","closed_at":"2025-12-13T11:21:15.714672548Z","compaction_level":0}
